data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T171718-eda49ba3/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T171718-eda49ba3/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T171718-eda49ba3/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T171718-eda49ba3/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T171718-eda49ba3/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-ae8787d7">
    <title>Add DDx guardrails for Fizeau routing boundary and agent-term removal</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
After specs and implementation remove DDx-owned `agent` vocabulary and routing defaults, the repo needs mechanical guardrails so future changes do not reintroduce DDx-side routing, fuzzy model matching, `agent.*` defaults, or new `ddx agent`/`cli/internal/agent` surfaces.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
Existing guardrail bead ddx-0ca4ffda only targets Profile/Harness/Model assignment on Runtime structs. The finalized plan is broader: DDx must not normalize model strings such as `qwen36`, must not inspect Fizeau catalogs for fuzzy matching, must not set model/provider/harness defaults from config, and must not add new DDx-owned `agent` packages or public commands. FEAT-006/FEAT-010 will define those constraints after ddx-424fdac4.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend routinglint/runtimelint or add a terminology lint so it catches: `ddx agent` public command additions, new `cli/internal/agent` package paths, DDx-side model normalization/fuzzy matching in run/try/work, DDx-side default Model/Provider/Harness config injection, and non-allowlisted `agent` terminology in DDx-owned execution packages/docs. Merge or supersede ddx-0ca4ffda as appropriate; keep explicit operator passthrough allowed.

NON-SCOPE
Do not block literal external-agent references, agentskills.io standard language, historical audit docs, or durable legacy keys that specs intentionally allowlist. Do not forbid explicit CLI passthrough flags. Do not implement Fizeau matching.

PARENT AND DEPENDENCIES
Parent: ddx-f088a3fd. Depends on spec update, Fizeau release integration, public CLI removal, and internal rename.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Guardrail tests fail on a fixture that adds a new `ddx agent` command or a new DDx-owned `cli/internal/agent` package.
2. Guardrail tests fail on a fixture that normalizes or fuzzy-matches `ServiceExecuteRequest.Model` in DDx run/try/work code.
3. Guardrail tests fail on config-driven default `Harness`, `Provider`, or `Model` injection into Fizeau Execute requests, while allowing explicit operator passthrough flags.
4. Guardrail tests allow audited literal external-agent terms and durable legacy storage keys through an explicit allowlist.
5. Existing ddx-0ca4ffda is updated, closed as superseded, or cross-referenced so there is one authoritative guardrail plan.
6. `cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/...` passes.
7. `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
8. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
9. Guardrail coverage from superseded ddx-0ca4ffda is preserved: TestRoutingLint_RunPathNeverSetsProfileDirectly (or a successor with the same invariant) fails on direct Profile/Harness/Model assignment on run/try/work Runtime structs, while explicit operator passthrough remains allowed.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
Folded narrow routinglint invariant from ddx-0ca4ffda into this canonical DDx/Fizeau routing-boundary guardrail bead. ddx-0ca4ffda can be made non-dispatchable as superseded.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, area:lint, area:execution, area:fizeau, kind:guardrail, prevention</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f088a3fd" spec-id="FEAT-006" base-rev="fc18015649b80d8de63edeeb54b5713e79cacdd8" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T171718-eda49ba3"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-006" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md">Feature: DDx Consumer of the Fizeau Execution Contract</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `aeede19c9` with subject `feat(routinglint): guard DDx routing boundary regressions [ddx-ae8787d7]`.

What changed:
- Extended `routinglint` with structural checks for:
  - a new `Use: "agent"` Cobra command surface
  - new `cli/internal/agent/*` subpackages outside the approved leaf set
  - `ServiceExecuteRequest` values that normalize or fuzzy-match `Harness`, `Provider`, or `Model`
  - `ServiceExecuteRequest` values sourced from config accessors instead of explicit passthrough values
- Added fixture coverage for the new failure cases and the allowlisted historical terms in `[analyzer_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ae8787d7-20260512T171718-eda49ba3/cli/tools/lint/routinglint/analyzer_test.go#L12)` and `[violations.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ae8787d7-20260512T171718-eda49ba3/cli/tools/lint/routinglint/testdata/src/violations/violations.go)`.
- Added explicit allowlist fixtures for historical literals and docs terms in `[clean.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ae8787d7-20260512T171718-eda49ba3/cli/tools/lint/routinglint/testdata/src/clean/clean.go)`.
- Updated the routing-lint docs in `[routing-lint.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-ae8787d7-20260512T171718-eda49ba3/docs/dev/routing-lint.md#L33)` to describe the new structural guardrails and explicit allowlist.
- Updated the superseded bead `ddx-0ca4ffda` notes to mark this as the canonical guardrail bead.

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./tools/lint/routinglint/...`
- `cd cli && go test ./...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

All passed.
